sections.prompt: Complete the sentences below with the following words and phrases in their right forms:

questions.prompt: In 2013 there were thought to be roughly 100 uncontacted ____________ worldwide, half of whom live in the Amazon rainforest.
His language was ____________. It appealed to the masses.
The old house ____________ memories of his childhood.
He changed the painting so much that the original was no longer ____________.
The ____________ had all the influence in government and the daily lives of the people.
He continued to expand his kingdom by ____________.
The highlands in Scotland were populated mainly by ____________ farmers.
Computerization has enabled us to ____________ a lot of paperwork.
He had a passive ____________ on his face.
That is a family that proceeds from a long line of ____________.
He said his tastes were ____________ because he only listened to classical music.

teacher.prompt: Watch the first part of the video then discuss the following questions with a partner or your teacher:

questions.prompt: Who would say 'hearty welcome'?
Who would say 'cordial reception'?
questions.sample_answer: Speakers in more formal, refined settings.

self.prompt: Watch the first part of the video then type a response to the following questions:

questions.prompt: Explain who would say 'hearty welcome' and why:
questions.sample_answer: Speakers in more informal situations might use 'hearty welcome'.

questions.prompt: Explain who would say 'cordial reception' and why:
questions.sample_answer: Speakers in more formal, refined situations might use 'cordial reception'.

sections.prompt: Answer the questions based on the video.

questions.prompt: What did the Romans protect the Celts and Britons from?
What was the name of the Germanic language that arose from the tribes that settled in Britain?
When did the Vikings invade Great Britain?
When was the Norman Conquest?
How was society divided after the Norman Conquest?

correct: Between French-speaking aristocracy and Old English speaking peasants
acceptable: French-speaking aristocracy and Old English speaking peasants

questions.prompt: In which three main areas did the French spoken by the Normans influence the English language the most?
Why did Anglo-Saxon words seem so 'plain'?

teacher.prompt: Discuss the following questions with a partner or with your teacher:

questions.prompt: Were you right about the types of people who would say 'hearty welcome' and 'cordial reception'?
Why do we perceive the two phrases differently?
Can you think of other examples of English words that come from French?
In what ways can your native language also have similar distinctions based on the words people use?

self.prompt: Type some responses to the following questions:

questions.prompt: Why do we perceive the two phrases 'hearty welcome' and 'cordial reception' differently?
In what ways can your native language also have similar distinctions based on the words people use?
